title: 5.3.1 Shortest Path and Applications
subtitle: Path problem modeling and extension-ready visualization/code/practice

content.introduction: Shortest-path problems optimize route cost under edge-weight models and appear in navigation, networking, and planning systems.

sections.title: 5.3.1.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define path, path cost, single-source and all-pairs shortest-path problem variants.
examples: Model city routing with weighted edges
Explain why negative cycles break finite shortest paths

sections.title: 5.3.1.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe problem setup assumptions including non-negative weights, negative edges, and graph directionality.
examples: Choose source set and distance initialization strategy
Interpret predecessor structure for route reconstruction

sections.title: 5.3.1.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Compare algorithm families by constraints: Dijkstra for non-negative, Bellman-Ford for negatives, Floyd for all-pairs.
examples: Select Dijkstra for sparse non-negative graphs
Select Bellman-Ford when negative edges exist
Select Floyd for dense all-pairs analysis

keyTakeaways: Set correct assumptions before selecting algorithms
Interpret path cost and predecessor results
Map constraints to suitable algorithm families
Apply shortest-path models in real systems
